MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Jake Schulte, Esq. 619-325-0492 Nicholas & Tomasevic for Plaintiff.
Korey DeClark, Esq. 805-459-8924 David Kwasniewski, Esq. 415-963-4460 for Defendant.

ARGUED. The Court hears Lines 11 (626318) and 12 (626319) simultaneously. Mr. Schulte requests TR should be confirmed; other side ignored court order, improper, states timeline, has nothing to do with Prop. 65, takes it personally - false, egregious, introduction is the same. Court poses questions. Mr. Clark responds and presents argument and chronology; misunderstanding of Judge Feng's order. Mr. Clark gives a copy of the transcript to the court and reads excerpt on page 6 line 13; addresses sanction. After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

1 - PLAINTIFF KATHERINE TYLER's MOTION TO STRIKE.

Plaintiff's motion to strike the introduction in the "Second Amended General Denial" is granted without leave to amend. Per CCP 436(a), the court may "[s]trike out any irrelevant, false, or improper matter" inserted in the answer/denial. As the court previously ruled, the statements regarding defendant's business ("delicious food"), plaintiffs' counsel's work on a sex exploitation case, prior Proposition 65 litigation, etc. are legally irrelevant to the germane issues in this case. Since the introduction (an attempt to sully plaintiff and counsel) is unnecessary, the court strikes the allegations without leave to amend.

Plaintiff correctly notes that "anyone can bring a Proposition 65 lawsuit against the seller of products containing chemicals that may cause cancer or reproductive toxicity without purchasing anything from the seller or suffering any individual harm." (Consumer Advocacy Group, Inc. v. Walmart, Inc. (2025) 112 Cal.App.5th 679, 689). While Proposition 65 vindicates public rights, there is no "public interest" defense nor can relief be denied because plaintiff's counsel allegedly engaged in prior misconduct. (Id.; Health & Safety Code 25249.7(d).) The issue is whether defendant's product contains a listed chemical and whether a proper warning was issued. The Attorney General's review of the certificate of merit vets whether the claim is in the public interest. (Health & Safety Code 25249.7(d), (e).) The information in the introduction is unnecessary for the court to determine if a subsequent settlement (if any) is in the public interest/fair.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and comply with CRC 3.1312. Party to drop a hard copy of the proposed order in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/REQ)

Jake Schulte, Esq. 619-325-0492 Nicholas & Tomasevic for Plaintiff.
Korey DeClark, Esq. 805-459-8924 David Kwasniewski, Esq. 415-963-4460 for Defendant.

Mr. Clark gives a copy of the transcript to the court and reads excerpt on page 6 line 13; addresses sanction. After full argument, the Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.

2 - PLAINTIFF KATHERINE TYLER's Motion For Sanctions.

Plaintiff's motion for sanctions is granted. (CCP 128.5; 128.7.) Defendant's insistence on including irrelevant and besmirching allegations in its denial is frivolous and a waste of counsel and court resources. The introduction of the "Second Amended General Denial" violated this court's prior order and there was no legal basis to include the allegations. Plaintiff complied with the safe harbor provision for sanctions and prior to this hearing, defendant did not seek leave to file an amended pleading or withdraw the Second Amended General Denial. Counsel Kwasniewski shall remit $5,000 as reasonable sanctions to plaintiff by March 2, 2026. Counsel shall self-report this sanctions order to the State Bar by March 2, 2026.

Counsel for the prevailing party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and comply with CRC 3.1312. Party to drop a hard copy of the proposed order in Dept. 302's inbox.
